model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Duke is the pick because its full-season profile has been the strongest in the country, with elite efficiency on both ends and the best defensive baseline in the available ratings. UConn has a credible path if its shot-making and tournament experience turn this into a late-game toss-up, but Duke’s higher overall floor makes it the safer choice. Caleb Foster’s injury lowers confidence some, yet not enough to outweigh the Blue Devils’ season-long edge.

reasoningStep.title: Duke over UConn on stronger two-way profile
reasoningStep.summary: Duke’s season-long metrics and defensive floor give it the edge over a dangerous UConn team whose upset path is real but narrower.
evidence: Duke is No. 1 in the available KenPom and Torvik data, with elite top-five offense and No. 1-level defense.
KenPom returned Duke at AdjEM 39.17, AdjO 127.9 and AdjD 88.7; Torvik returned Duke at 31-2 with AdjOE 128.0 and AdjDE 90.45.
Recent reporting indicates Duke guard Caleb Foster is out indefinitely with a foot fracture, which trims some backcourt margin but does not erase Duke’s overall edge.
UConn’s credible upset path comes from its ceiling, shot-making, and tournament-tested core, but no stronger availability or statistical edge surfaced to move the pick off Duke.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: demo-2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: e8-east-1
Current game label: East Regional Final
Round: Elite 8
Resolved teams: Duke (duke) vs UConn (uconn)
Prior committed picks: 60

result.snippet: UConn hosts Providence on Sunday and then has road games against Villanova and Georgetown. The Blue Demons rallied after McNeeley's injury, but the Huskies got big 3-pointers from Alex Karaban and Solo Ball to help close it out. "Little bit of a mishap, but it's just the next man up mentality," Ball said of McNeeley's departure.
